name: Wizard
description: A scholarly magic-user capable of manipulating the structures of reality through studied arcane mastery. Wizards learn spells by recording them in a spellbook, and their power grows through dedicated study and preparation.

features.name: Proficiencies
features.description: Your Wizard training grants the following proficiencies.

features.name: Spellcasting
features.description:
As a student of arcane magic, you have learned to cast spells.

**Cantrips.** You know three Wizard cantrips of your choice. {spell:Light}, {spell:Mage Hand}, and {spell:Ray of Frost} are recommended. Whenever you finish a {rule:Long Rest}, you can replace one of your cantrips from this feature with another Wizard cantrip of your choice. When you reach Wizard levels 4 and 10, you learn another Wizard cantrip of your choice, as shown in the Cantrips column of the Wizard Features table.

**Spellbook.** Your wizardly apprenticeship culminated in the creation of a unique book: your spellbook. It is a Tiny object that weighs 3 pounds, contains 100 pages, and can be read only by you or someone casting {spell:Identify}. The book contains the level 1+ spells you know. It starts with six level 1 Wizard spells of your choice. {spell:Detect Magic}, {spell:Feather Fall}, {spell:Mage Armor}, {spell:Magic Missile}, {spell:Sleep}, and {spell:Thunderwave} are recommended. Whenever you gain a Wizard level after 1, add two Wizard spells of your choice to your spellbook. Each of these spells must be of a level for which you have spell slots, as shown in the Wizard Features table.

**Spell Slots.** The Wizard Features table shows how many spell slots you have to cast your level 1+ spells. You regain all expended slots when you finish a {rule:Long Rest}.

**Prepared Spells of Level 1+.** You prepare the list of level 1+ spells that are available for you to cast with this feature. To do so, choose four spells from your spellbook. The chosen spells must be of a level for which you have spell slots. The number of spells on your list increases as you gain Wizard levels, as shown in the Prepared Spells column of the Wizard Features table. If another Wizard feature gives you spells that you always have prepared, those spells don't count against the number of spells you can prepare with this feature, but those spells otherwise count as Wizard spells for you.

**Changing Your Prepared Spells.** Whenever you finish a {rule:Long Rest}, you can change your list of prepared spells, replacing any of the spells there with spells from your spellbook.

**Spellcasting Ability.** Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for your Wizard spells.

**Spellcasting Focus.** You can use an {item:Arcane Focus} or your spellbook as a {rule:Spellcasting Focus} for your Wizard spells.

features.name: Ritual Adept
features.description: You can cast any spell as a {rule:Ritual} if that spell has the Ritual tag and the spell is in your spellbook. You needn't have the spell prepared, but you must read from the book to cast a spell in this way.

properties.text: You can cast any Ritual-tagged spell from your spellbook as a Ritual (10 extra minutes, no slot consumed) without having it prepared. You must have the spellbook in hand.

features.name: Arcane Recovery
features.description:
You can regain some of your magical energy by studying your spellbook. When you finish a {rule:Short Rest}, you can choose expended spell slots to recover. The spell slots can have a combined level equal to no more than half your Wizard level (round up), and none of the slots can be level 6 or higher. For example, if you're a level 4 Wizard, you can recover up to two levels' worth of spell slots, regaining either one level 2 spell slot or two level 1 spell slots.

Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a {rule:Long Rest}.

properties.text: On a Short Rest, recover expended spell slots with combined level up to ceil(Wizard level / 2). No slot recovered can be level 6 or higher.

features.name: Scholar
features.description: While studying magic, you also specialized in another field of study. Choose one of the following skills in which you have proficiency: {rule:Arcana|Arcana}, {rule:History|History}, {rule:Investigation|Investigation}, {rule:Medicine|Medicine}, {rule:Nature|Nature}, or {rule:Religion|Religion}. You have {rule:Expertise} in the chosen skill.

features.name: Wizard Subclass
features.description: You gain a Wizard subclass of your choice. A subclass is a specialization that grants you features at certain Wizard levels. For the rest of your career, you gain each of your subclass's features that are of your Wizard level or lower.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the {feat:Ability Score Improvement} feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. You gain this feature again at Wizard levels 8, 12, and 16.

features.name: Memorize Spell
features.description: Whenever you finish a {rule:Short Rest}, you can study your spellbook and replace one of the level 1+ Wizard spells you have prepared for your Spellcasting feature with another level 1+ spell from the book.

properties.text: After each Short Rest, you can swap one prepared level 1+ Wizard spell for another level 1+ spell from your spellbook.

features.name: Subclass Feature
features.description: You gain a feature from your Wizard Subclass.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the {feat:Ability Score Improvement} feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Subclass Feature
features.description: You gain a feature from your Wizard Subclass.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the {feat:Ability Score Improvement} feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Subclass Feature
features.description: You gain a feature from your Wizard Subclass.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the {feat:Ability Score Improvement} feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Spell Mastery
features.description:
You have achieved such mastery over certain spells that you can cast them at will. Choose a level 1 and a level 2 spell in your spellbook that have a casting time of an action. You always have those spells prepared, and you can cast them at their lowest level without expending a spell slot. To cast either spell at a higher level, you must expend a spell slot.

Whenever you finish a {rule:Long Rest}, you can study your spellbook and replace one of those spells with an eligible spell of the same level from the book.

features.name: Epic Boon
features.description: You gain an Epic Boon feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. {feat:Boon of Spell Recall} is recommended.

features.name: Signature Spells
features.description: Choose two level 3 spells in your spellbook as your signature spells. You always have these spells prepared, and you can cast each of them once at level 3 without expending a spell slot. When you do so, you can't cast them in this way again until you finish a {rule:Short Rest} or {rule:Long Rest}. To cast either spell at a higher level, you must expend a spell slot.

properties.text: Choose two level 3 Wizard spells from your spellbook as signature spells. They are always prepared (free). Each can be cast once at level 3 without a spell slot, resetting on Short or Long Rest. The 2-use resource tracks both charges independently — one per spell.

subclasses.name: Abjurer
subclasses.description: Your study of magic is focused on spells that block, banish, or protect — ending harmful effects, banishing evil influences, and protecting the weak. Abjurers are sought when baleful spirits require exorcism, when locations must be guarded against magical spying, and when portals to other planes of existence must be closed. Adventuring parties value Abjurers for the protection they provide against a variety of hostile magic and other attacks.

features.name: Abjuration Savant
features.description:
Choose two Wizard spells from the Abjuration school, each of which must be no higher than level 2, and add them to your spellbook for free.

In addition, whenever you gain access to a new level of spell slots in this class, you can add one Wizard spell from the Abjuration school to your spellbook for free. The chosen spell must be of a level for which you have spell slots.

properties.text: Choose 2 Abjuration Wizard spells of level 1–2 and copy them into your spellbook for free. Each time you gain access to a new spell slot level, also copy one Abjuration Wizard spell of that level or lower into your spellbook for free.

features.name: Arcane Ward
features.description:
You can weave magic around yourself for protection. When you cast an Abjuration spell with a spell slot, you can simultaneously use a strand of the spell's magic to create a magical ward on yourself that lasts until you finish a {rule:Long Rest}. The ward has a Hit Point maximum equal to twice your Wizard level plus your Intelligence modifier. Whenever you take damage, the ward takes the damage instead, and if you have any Resistances or Vulnerabilities, apply them before reducing the ward's Hit Points. If the damage reduces the ward to 0 Hit Points, you take any remaining damage. While the ward has 0 Hit Points, it can't absorb damage, but its magic remains.

Whenever you cast an Abjuration spell with a spell slot, the ward regains a number of Hit Points equal to twice the level of the spell slot. Alternatively, as a {rule:Bonus Action}, you can expend a spell slot, and the ward regains a number of Hit Points equal to twice the level of the spell slot expended.

Once you create the ward, you can't create it again until you finish a {rule:Long Rest}.

properties.text: Arcane Ward max HP = (2 × Wizard level) + Intelligence modifier. Created when you cast an Abjuration spell with a spell slot. Absorbs all incoming damage before HP. Ward regains HP equal to twice the slot level each time you cast an Abjuration spell with a slot, or as a Bonus Action by expending a spell slot. Can only be created once per Long Rest.

features.name: Projected Ward
features.description: When a creature that you can see within 30 feet of yourself takes damage, you can take a {rule:Reaction} to cause your Arcane Ward to absorb that damage. If this damage reduces the ward to 0 Hit Points, the warded creature takes any remaining damage. If that creature has any Resistances or Vulnerabilities, apply them before reducing the ward's Hit Points.

properties.text: As a Reaction, redirect damage dealt to a creature within 30 feet into your Arcane Ward instead. Remaining damage after the ward reaches 0 HP falls through to the original target (applying that target's own resistances/vulnerabilities first).

features.name: Spell Breaker
features.description:
You always have the {spell:Counterspell} and {spell:Dispel Magic} spells prepared. In addition, you can cast {spell:Dispel Magic} as a {rule:Bonus Action}, and you can add your Proficiency Bonus to its ability check.

When you cast either spell with a spell slot, that slot isn't expended if the spell fails to stop a spell.

properties.text: Dispel Magic can be cast as a Bonus Action. Add Proficiency Bonus to Dispel Magic's ability check. When Counterspell or Dispel Magic fails to stop a spell, the spell slot used is not expended.

features.name: Spell Resistance
features.description: You have {rule:Advantage} on saving throws against spells, and you have {rule:Resistance|resistance} to the damage of spells.

subclasses.name: Diviner
subclasses.description: The counsel of a Diviner is sought by those who want a clearer understanding of the past, present, and future. As a Diviner, you strive to part the veils of space, time, and consciousness. You work to master spells of discernment, remote viewing, supernatural knowledge, and foresight.

features.name: Divination Savant
features.description:
Choose two Wizard spells from the Divination school, each of which must be no higher than level 2, and add them to your spellbook for free.

In addition, whenever you gain access to a new level of spell slots in this class, you can add one Wizard spell from the Divination school to your spellbook for free. The chosen spell must be of a level for which you have spell slots.

properties.text: Choose 2 Divination Wizard spells of level 1–2 and copy them into your spellbook for free. Each time you gain access to a new spell slot level, also copy one Divination Wizard spell of that level or lower into your spellbook for free.

features.name: Portent
features.description:
Glimpses of the future begin to press on your awareness. Whenever you finish a {rule:Long Rest}, roll two d20s and record the numbers rolled. You can replace any {rule:D20 Test} made by you or a creature that you can see with one of these foretelling rolls. You must choose to do so before the roll, and you can replace a roll in this way only once per turn.

Each foretelling roll can be used only once. When you finish a {rule:Long Rest}, you lose any unused foretelling rolls.

properties.text: On Long Rest, roll 2d20 and record both. Before any D20 Test by you or a visible creature, substitute one Portent die for the roll. Only one substitution per turn. Unused dice expire on Long Rest.

features.name: Expert Divination
features.description: Casting Divination spells comes so easily to you that it expends only a fraction of your spellcasting efforts. When you cast a Divination spell using a level 2+ spell slot, you regain one expended spell slot. The slot you regain must be of a level lower than the slot you expended and can't be higher than level 5.

properties.text: When you cast a Divination spell using a level 2+ spell slot, regain one expended spell slot of a lower level (max level 5).

features.name: The Third Eye
features.description:
You can increase your powers of perception. As a {rule:Bonus Action}, choose one of the following benefits, which lasts until you start a {rule:Short Rest} or {rule:Long Rest}. You can't use this feature again until you finish a {rule:Short Rest} or {rule:Long Rest}.

**Darkvision.** You gain {rule:Darkvision} with a range of 120 feet.

**Greater Comprehension.** You can read any language.

**See Invisibility.** You can cast {spell:See Invisibility} without expending a spell slot.

properties.text: As a Bonus Action, activate one of three benefits lasting until your next Short or Long Rest: Darkvision (120 ft), Greater Comprehension (read any language), or See Invisibility (cast without spell slot). One use per Short or Long Rest.

features.name: Greater Portent
features.description: The visions in your dreams intensify and paint a more accurate picture in your mind of what is to come. Roll three d20s for your Portent feature rather than two.

properties.text: Your Portent feature now generates 3 Portent dice per Long Rest instead of 2.

subclasses.name: Evoker
subclasses.description: Your studies focus on magic that creates powerful elemental effects such as bitter cold, searing flame, rolling thunder, crackling lightning, and burning acid. Some Evokers find employment in military forces, serving as artillery to blast armies from afar. Others use their power to protect others, while some seek their own gain.

features.name: Evocation Savant
features.description:
Choose two Wizard spells from the Evocation school, each of which must be no higher than level 2, and add them to your spellbook for free.

In addition, whenever you gain access to a new level of spell slots in this class, you can add one Wizard spell from the Evocation school to your spellbook for free. The chosen spell must be of a level for which you have spell slots.

properties.text: Choose 2 Evocation Wizard spells of level 1–2 and copy them into your spellbook for free. Each time you gain access to a new spell slot level, also copy one Evocation Wizard spell of that level or lower into your spellbook for free.

features.name: Potent Cantrip
features.description: Your damaging cantrips affect even creatures that avoid the brunt of the effect. When you cast a cantrip at a creature and you miss with the attack roll or the target succeeds on a saving throw against the cantrip, the target takes half the cantrip's damage (if any) but suffers no additional effect from the cantrip.

properties.text: When you miss with a cantrip attack roll or a target succeeds on a saving throw against your cantrip, the target takes half the cantrip's damage (if any) but none of its other effects.

features.name: Sculpt Spells
features.description: You can create pockets of relative safety within the effects of your evocations. When you cast an Evocation spell that affects other creatures that you can see, you can choose a number of them equal to 1 plus the spell's level. The chosen creatures automatically succeed on their saving throws against the spell, and they take no damage if they would normally take half damage on a successful save.

properties.text: When you cast an Evocation spell, choose up to (1 + spell level) creatures you can see. Those creatures automatically succeed on saving throws against the spell and take no damage even if they would take half on a success.

features.name: Empowered Evocation
features.description: Whenever you cast a Wizard spell from the Evocation school, you can add your Intelligence modifier to one damage roll of that spell.

modifiers.condition: once per Evocation school Wizard spell cast

features.name: Overchannel
features.description:
You can increase the power of your spells. When you cast a Wizard spell with a spell slot of levels 1–5 that deals damage, you can deal maximum damage with that spell on the turn you cast it.

The first time you do so, you suffer no adverse effect. If you use this feature again before you finish a {rule:Long Rest}, you take 2d12 Necrotic damage for each level of the spell slot immediately after you cast it. This damage ignores {rule:Resistance} and {rule:Immunity}.

Each time you use this feature again before finishing a {rule:Long Rest}, the Necrotic damage per spell level increases by 1d12.

properties.text: When casting a levels 1–5 Wizard spell that deals damage, you can maximize all damage dice. First use per Long Rest has no cost. Each subsequent use before a Long Rest deals Necrotic damage equal to (2d12 × spell slot level) for the second use, increasing by 1d12 per spell level for each additional use. This Necrotic damage ignores Resistance and Immunity.

subclasses.name: Illusionist
subclasses.description: You specialize in magic that dazzles the senses and tricks the mind, and the illusions you craft make the impossible seem real.

features.name: Illusion Savant
features.description:
Choose two Wizard spells from the Illusion school, each of which must be no higher than level 2, and add them to your spellbook for free.

In addition, whenever you gain access to a new level of spell slots in this class, you can add one Wizard spell from the Illusion school to your spellbook for free. The chosen spell must be of a level for which you have spell slots.

properties.text: Choose 2 Illusion Wizard spells of level 1–2 and copy them into your spellbook for free. Each time you gain access to a new spell slot level, also copy one Illusion Wizard spell of that level or lower into your spellbook for free.

features.name: Improved Illusions
features.description:
You can cast Illusion spells without providing Verbal components, and if an Illusion spell you cast has a range of 10+ feet, the range increases by 60 feet.

You also know the {spell:Minor Illusion} cantrip. If you already know it, you learn a different Wizard cantrip of your choice. The cantrip doesn't count against your number of cantrips known. You can create both a sound and an image with a single casting of {spell:Minor Illusion}, and you can cast it as a {rule:Bonus Action}.

properties.text: Illusion spells you cast don't require Verbal components. If the Illusion spell has a range of 10+ feet, its range increases by 60 feet. Minor Illusion can produce both sound and image in one casting, and can be cast as a Bonus Action. If you already know Minor Illusion, learn a different Wizard cantrip instead.

features.name: Phantasmal Creatures
features.description: You always have the {spell:Summon Beast} and {spell:Summon Fey} spells prepared. Whenever you cast either spell, you can change its school to Illusion, which causes the summoned creature to appear spectral. You can cast the Illusion version of each spell without expending a spell slot, but casting it without a slot halves the creature's Hit Points. Once you cast either spell without a spell slot, you must finish a {rule:Long Rest} before you can cast the spell in that way again.

properties.text: Summon Beast and Summon Fey are always prepared. Either can be cast as an Illusion (spectral form, half HP) without expending a spell slot once per Long Rest each. When cast without a slot, the summoned creature has half its normal Hit Points.

features.name: Illusory Self
features.description:
When a creature hits you with an attack roll, you can take a {rule:Reaction} to interpose an illusory duplicate of yourself between the attacker and yourself. The attack automatically misses you, then the illusion dissipates.

Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {rule:Short Rest} or {rule:Long Rest}. You can also restore your use of it by expending a level 2+ spell slot (no action required).

properties.text: As a Reaction when a creature hits you with an attack, cause the attack to automatically miss. Recharges on Short or Long Rest, or by expending a level 2+ spell slot (no action required).

features.name: Illusory Reality
features.description: You have learned to weave shadow magic into your illusions to give them a semi-reality. When you cast an Illusion spell with a spell slot, you can choose one inanimate, nonmagical object that is part of the illusion and make that object real. You can do this on your turn as a {rule:Bonus Action} while the spell is ongoing. The object remains real for 1 minute, during which it can't deal damage or give any conditions. For example, you can create an illusion of a bridge over a chasm and then make it real and cross it.

properties.text: When you cast an Illusion spell with a spell slot, you can as a Bonus Action on your turn make one inanimate nonmagical object within the illusion real for 1 minute. The real object can't deal damage or impose conditions.
